---
title: "XGBoost Model"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```


```{r Libraries_and_Data}

# In General
library(dplyr)

# Model
library(xgboost)
library(mltools)
library(tidymodels)
library(themis)     # step_downsample()
library(doParallel) # Parallel Cluster

# Model Significance + Visualizations + Testing Variable Importance
library(vip)
library(shapviz)
library(DALEXtra) # Extension for tidymodels integration
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(yardstick)
library(purrr)
library(ggnewscale)
library(forcats)
library(here)

# Importing & Preparing Data
incident_data <- read.csv(here("output", "clustered_incident_data.csv"))

```


```{r Preparing_Model_t}

xgb_data_t <- incident_data %>%
  select(!1:7 & !9:10) %>%
  mutate(crime_weight = importance_weights(crime_weight)) %>%
  mutate(outcome = as.factor(outcome)) %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.factor(cluster))

str(xgb_data_t)


set.seed(12345)
data_split_t <- initial_split(xgb_data_t, prop = 0.8, strata = outcome)
train_data_t <- training(data_split_t)
test_data_t  <- testing(data_split_t)

cv_folds_t <- vfold_cv(train_data_t, v = 5, strata = outcome)

xgb_specs_t <- boost_tree(trees = 1000,
                          tree_depth = 4,
                          learn_rate = 0.05,
                          mtry = 15,
                          min_n = 10,
                          stop_iter = 30) %>%
               set_engine("xgboost", event_level = "second",
                          tree_method = "hist",
                          nthread = parallel::detectCores() - 1) %>%
               set_mode("classification")

xgb_recipe_t <- recipe(outcome ~ ., data = train_data_t) %>%
  step_dummy(cluster) %>%
  step_downsample(outcome, under_ratio = 1.5)

xgb_workflow_t <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(xgb_recipe_t) %>%
  add_model(xgb_specs_t)

```


```{r Fitting_Model_t}

# Start The Parallel Cluster
cl <- makePSOCKcluster(parallel::detectCores() - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

set.seed(12345)
cv_results_t <- fit_resamples(xgb_workflow_t,
                              resamples = cv_folds_t,                  
                              metrics = metric_set(f_meas, yardstick::mcc),
                              control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE))

final_workflow_t <- finalize_workflow(xgb_workflow_t,
                                      select_best(cv_results_t,
                                                  metric = "f_meas"))

final_results_t <- last_fit(final_workflow_t,
                            split = data_split_t,
                            metrics = metric_set(f_meas, yardstick::mcc))

# Stop The Parallel Cluster
stopCluster(cl)

```


```{r Model_Performance_t}

model_metrics_t = collect_metrics(final_results_t, summarize = TRUE)
print(model_metrics_t)
collect_predictions(final_results_t) %>%
  conf_mat(truth = outcome, estimate = .pred_class)

```


```{r Confusion_Matrix_t}

ibm_magenta <- "#d81b60" 
ibm_blue    <- "#5c92ff"

cm_object_t <- conf_mat(collect_predictions(final_results_t), truth = outcome, estimate = .pred_class)
cm_data_t <- as.data.frame(cm_object_t$table)
colnames(cm_data_t) <- c("Estimate", "Truth", "Count")

cm_data_t <- cm_data_t %>%
  mutate(Estimate = fct_rev(as.factor(Estimate)),
         Truth = as.factor(Truth), 
         correct = (as.character(Estimate) == as.character(Truth)),
         pct = Count / sum(Count),
         pct_text = paste0("(", label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(pct), ")"),
         class_label = case_when(
           Truth == "1" & Estimate == "1" ~ "True Positive",
           Truth == "0" & Estimate == "1" ~ "False Positive",
           Truth == "1" & Estimate == "0" ~ "False Negative",
           Truth == "0" & Estimate == "0" ~ "True Negative"),
         main_label = paste0(comma(Count), "\n", pct_text))

ggplot(cm_data_t, aes(x = Truth, y = Estimate)) +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_data_t,
                          correct),
            aes(fill = Count),
            color = "white",
            linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F5E6FF",
                      high = ibm_magenta,
                      guide = "none") +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_data_t, !correct),
            aes(fill = Count),
            color = "white",
            linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#E8F0FF",
                      high = ibm_blue,
                      guide = "none") +
  geom_text(aes(label = main_label),
            size = 3.8,
            fontface = "bold",
            lineheight = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = class_label),
            vjust = 4.5,
            size = 2.6,
            color = "gray20") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Violent", "0" = "Property")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Violent", "0" = "Property")) +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix: Crime Type",
       x = "Actual Class", 
       y = "Predicted Class") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 16,
                                  margin = margin(b = 4)),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40",
                                     size = 11,
                                     margin = margin(b = 15)),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 13),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30",
                                 size = 11,
                                 face = "italic"),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA,
                                        color = "gray90",
                                        linewidth = 0.5))


```


```{r Prepare_For_Data_Visualizations_t}

final_xgb_t <- extract_fit_engine(final_results_t)
trained_workflow_t <- extract_workflow(final_results_t)

set.seed(123)
sampled_test_t <- test_data_t %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_sample(prop = 1000 / nrow(test_data_t)) %>%
  ungroup()

features_sample_t <- trained_workflow_t %>%
  extract_recipe() %>%
  bake(new_data = sampled_test_t, all_predictors()) %>%
  as.matrix()

```


```{r SHAP_t, fig.height = 6, fig.width = 8}

shp_t <- shapviz(final_xgb_t, X_pred = features_sample_t, interactions = FALSE)

shp_beeswarm_t <- sv_importance(shp_t,
                                kind = "beeswarm",
                                show_numbers = TRUE,
                                max_display = 22) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        breaks = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("Low", "High"),
                        name = "Feature Value") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(-0.8, 0.8),
    breaks = seq(-0.8, 0.8, by = 0.4)) +
  labs(title = "SHAP Values Summary",
       subtitle = "Socio-economic features on crime classification",
       x = "Property Crime   -{ SHAP Value }-    Violent Crime",
       y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 14,
                                  margin = margin(b = 5)),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color =
                                            "gray90",
                                          linewidth = 0.5),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray95"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black",
                                   face = "italic"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10,
                                    face = "bold"))

shp_beeswarm_t

```


```{r Violent_Permutation}

custom_violent_f1_loss <- function(observed, predicted) {
  obs_factor  <- factor(observed, levels = c(0, 1))
  pred_factor <- factor(ifelse(predicted >= 0.5, 1, 0), levels = c(0, 1))
  
  f1 <- yardstick::f_meas_vec(obs_factor, pred_factor, event_level = "second")
  
  return(1 - f1)
}

set.seed(12345)
violent_explainer_xgb <- explain_tidymodels(
  model = trained_workflow_t,
  data = train_data_t %>% select(-outcome),
  y = as.numeric(train_data_t$outcome == "1"),
  label = "XGBoost Model: Violent Crime Predictors",
  verbose = FALSE
)

set.seed(12345)
violent_vip_permutation <- model_parts(
  explainer = violent_explainer_xgb,
  loss_function = custom_violent_f1_loss,
  B = 5
)

```


```{r Property_Permutation}

custom_property_f1_loss <- function(observed, predicted) {
  obs_factor  <- factor(observed, levels = c(0, 1))
  pred_factor <- factor(ifelse(predicted >= 0.5, 1, 0), levels = c(0, 1))
  
  f1 <- yardstick::f_meas_vec(obs_factor, pred_factor, event_level = "first")
  
  return(1 - f1)
}

set.seed(12345)
property_explainer_xgb <- explain_tidymodels(
  model = trained_workflow_t,
  data = train_data_t %>% select(-outcome),
  y = as.numeric(train_data_t$outcome == "0"),
  label = "XGBoost Model: Property Crime Predictors",
  verbose = FALSE
)

set.seed(12345)
property_vip_permutation <- model_parts(
  explainer = property_explainer_xgb,
  loss_function = custom_property_f1_loss,
  B = 5
)

```


```{r Violent_Permutation_Plot}

violent_vip_p <- plot(violent_vip_permutation)

violent_plot <- violent_vip_p +
  labs(title = "Permutation Feature Importance for Violent Crimes",
       subtitle = "Model performance degradation measured via unweighted Macro F1-Score",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Loss (1 - F1) After Variable Permutation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 14,
                                  margin = margin(b = 5)),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90",
                                          linewidth = 0.5),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray95"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black",
                                   face = "italic"),
        legend.position = "none")

print(violent_plot)

```


```{r Property_Permutation_Plot}

property_p <- plot(property_vip_permutation)

property_plot <- property_p +
  labs(title = "Permutation Feature Importance for Property Crimes",
       subtitle = "Model performance degradation measured via unweighted Macro F1-Score",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Loss (1 - F1) After Variable Permutation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 14,
                                  margin = margin(b = 5)),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90",
                                          linewidth = 0.5),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray95"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black",
                                   face = "italic"),
        legend.position = "none")

print(property_plot)

```




```{r Preparing_Model_st}

xgb_data_st <- incident_data %>%
  select(!1:8 & !10) %>%
  mutate(crime_weight = importance_weights(crime_weight)) %>%
  mutate(outcome_subtype = as.factor(outcome_subtype)) %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.factor(cluster))

str(xgb_data_st)

set.seed(12345)
data_split_st <- initial_split(xgb_data_st,
                               prop = 0.8,
                               strata = outcome_subtype)
train_data_st <- training(data_split_st)
test_data_st  <- testing(data_split_st)

cv_folds_st <- vfold_cv(train_data_st, v = 5, strata = outcome_subtype)

xgb_specs_st <- boost_tree(trees = 1000,
                           tree_depth = 4,
                           learn_rate = 0.05,
                           mtry = 15,
                           min_n = 10,
                           stop_iter = 30) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost",
             tree_method = "hist",
             nthread = parallel::detectCores() - 1) %>% 
  set_mode("classification")

xgb_recipe_st <- recipe(outcome_subtype ~ ., data = train_data_st) %>%
  step_dummy(cluster) %>%
  step_downsample(outcome_subtype, under_ratio = 10.0)

xgb_workflow_st <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(xgb_recipe_st) %>%
  add_model(xgb_specs_st)

multiclass_metrics <- metric_set(f_meas, yardstick::mcc)

```


```{r Fitting_Model_st}

# Start The Parallel Cluster
cl <- makePSOCKcluster(parallel::detectCores() - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

set.seed(12345)
cv_results_st <- fit_resamples(xgb_workflow_st,
                               resamples = cv_folds_st,
                               metrics = multiclass_metrics,
                               estimator = "macro",
                               control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE))

final_workflow_st <- finalize_workflow(xgb_workflow_st,
                                       select_best(cv_results_st,
                                                   metric = "f_meas"))

final_results_st <- last_fit(final_workflow_st,
                             split = data_split_st,
                             metrics = multiclass_metrics,
                             estimator = "macro")

# Stop The Parallel Cluster
stopCluster(cl)

```


```{r Model_Performance_st}

model_metrics_st = collect_metrics(final_results_st, summarize = TRUE)
print(model_metrics_st)
collect_predictions(final_results_st) %>%
  conf_mat(truth = outcome_subtype, estimate = .pred_class)

```


```{r Confusion_Matrix_st, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

ibm_magenta <- "#d81b60" 
ibm_blue    <- "#5c92ff"

cm_object_st <- conf_mat(collect_predictions(final_results_st), truth = outcome_subtype, estimate = .pred_class)
cm_data_st <- as.data.frame(cm_object_st$table)
colnames(cm_data_st) <- c("Estimate", "Truth", "Count")

cm_data_st <- cm_data_st %>%
  mutate(Estimate = fct_rev(as.factor(Estimate)),
         Truth = as.factor(Truth), 
         correct = (as.character(Estimate) == as.character(Truth)),
         pct = Count / sum(Count),
         pct_text = label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(pct),
         main_label = if_else(Count > 0, paste0(comma(Count),
                                                "\n(", pct_text, ")"), "-"))

ggplot(cm_data_st, aes(x = Truth, y = Estimate)) +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_data_st, correct),
            aes(fill = Count),
            color = "white",
            linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F5E6FF",
                      high = ibm_magenta,
                      guide = "none") +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_data_st, !correct),
            aes(fill = Count),
            color = "white",
            linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#E8F0FF",
                      high = ibm_blue,
                      guide = "none") +
  geom_text(aes(label = main_label),
            size = 2.5,
            fontface = "bold",
            lineheight = 1.1) +
  labs(title = "Multiclass Confusion Matrix: Crime Subtypes",
       x = "Actual Class", 
       y = "Predicted Class") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 16,
                                  margin = margin(b = 4)),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40",
                                     size = 11,
                                     margin = margin(b = 15)),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                  size = 13),
        axis.text.x = element_text(color = "gray30",
                                   size = 11,
                                   face = "italic",
                                   angle = 45,
                                   hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "gray30",
                                   size = 11,
                                   face = "italic"),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA,
                                        color = "gray90",
                                        linewidth = 0.5))

```


```{r Prepare_For_Data_Visualizations_st}

final_xgb_st <- extract_fit_engine(final_results_st)
trained_workflow_st <- extract_workflow(final_results_st)

set.seed(123)
sampled_test_st <- test_data_st %>%
  group_by(outcome_subtype) %>%
  slice_sample(prop = 1000 / nrow(test_data_st)) %>%
  ungroup()

features_sample_st <- trained_workflow_st %>%
  extract_recipe() %>%
  bake(new_data = sampled_test_st, all_predictors()) %>%
  as.matrix()

```


```{r SHAP_st}


shp_st <- shapviz(final_xgb_st, X_pred = features_sample_st, interactions = FALSE)
labels_st <- levels(as.factor(train_data_st$outcome_subtype))
names(shp_st) <- labels_st
all_classes <- names(shp_st)

for (current_class in all_classes) {
  single_class_shp <- shp_st[[current_class]]
  p_bee <- sv_importance(single_class_shp,
                         kind = "beeswarm",
                         show_numbers = TRUE,
                         max_display = 10) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                          breaks = c(0, 1),
                          labels = c("Low", "High"),
                          name = "Feature Value") +
    labs(title = paste("SHAP Feature Importance Beeswarm:", current_class),
         subtitle = "Isolated impact profiles on classification probability") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold",
                                    size = 14,
                                    margin = margin(b = 5)),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90",
                                            linewidth = 0.5),
          panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray95"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black",
                                     face = "italic"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 10,
                                      face = "bold"))
  print(p_bee)
}


```


```{r Testing_Variable_Importance_Subtype}

custom_f1_loss_multi <- function(observed, predicted) {
  obs_factor <- as.factor(observed)
  predicted_class_idx <- max.col(predicted, ties.method = "first")
  pred_factor         <- factor(levels(obs_factor)[predicted_class_idx],
                                levels = levels(obs_factor))
  f1_macro <- yardstick::f_meas_vec(obs_factor, pred_factor, estimator = "macro")
  return(1 - f1_macro)
}

multi_explainer_xgb <- explain_tidymodels(
  model = trained_workflow_st,
  data = train_data_st %>% select(!outcome_subtype),
  y = train_data_st$outcome_subtype, 
  label = "XGBoost Model: Crime Subtype Predictors",
  verbose = FALSE
)

multi_vip_permutation <- model_parts(
  explainer = multi_explainer_xgb,
  loss_function = custom_f1_loss_multi,
  B = 5
)

```


```{r Permutation_Plots_st}

class_levels <- levels(as.factor(train_data_st$outcome_subtype))

for (current_level in class_levels) {
  
  level_loss_func <- function(observed, predicted) {
    obs_binary <- factor(ifelse(observed == current_level, "Target", "Other"), 
                         levels = c("Target", "Other"))

    level_idx <- which(class_levels == current_level)
    level_probs <- predicted[, level_idx]
    
    predicted_class_idx <- max.col(predicted, ties.method = "first")
    pred_binary <- factor(ifelse(predicted_class_idx == level_idx, "Target", "Other"), 
                          levels = c("Target", "Other"))
    
    f1_level <- yardstick::f_meas_vec(obs_binary, pred_binary, event_level = "first")
    
    return(1 - f1_level)
  }
  
  multi_explainer_xgb$y <- as.factor(train_data_st$outcome_subtype)
  
  level_vip <- model_parts(
    explainer = multi_explainer_xgb,
    loss_function = level_loss_func,
    B = 5
  )
  
  p <- plot(level_vip)
  
  p_plot <- p +
    labs(
      title = paste("Permutation Feature Importance:", current_level),
      subtitle = paste("Model performance degradation measured via binary F1-Score for", current_level),
      y = NULL,
      x = "Loss (1 - F1) After Variable Permutation"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, margin = margin(b = 5)),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.5),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray95"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", face = "italic"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )

  print(p_plot)
}

```
